w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Provide citizens a unified platform to report civic issues such as potholes, garbage overflow, streetlight failures, and water leakage.
Enable live tracking of complaint status using
so users can instantly see progress without refreshing the page.
Automatically route complaints to the correct municipal department based on issue type and location.
Allow citizens to monitor resolution timelines and department responsiveness.
Provide analytics dashboards for administrators to identify high
risk zones and recurring urban problems.
The Smart City Incident Reporting System is a web

based civic engagement platform designed to bridge the communication gap between citizens and municipal authorities. Urban residents often face challenges in reporting infrastructure problems due to fragmented communication channels and lack of transparency in complaint resolution.
This system enables users to submit complaints by selecting a location on the map, uploading images, and categorizing the issue. The
backend handles authentication, complaint routing, and administrative workflows, while
ensures reliable storage of geospatial and complaint data.
powered by Django Channels, citizens receive real
time updates whenever the complaint status changes (e.g., Submitted → Assigned → In Progress → Resolved). Authorities can update progress from their dashboard, instantly notifying the concerned user.

Administrators also gain access to analytics dashboards showing heatmaps of complaints, department efficiency metrics, and average resolution time, enabling better urban planning and resource allocation.
Secure citizen registration and login system.
Users select the exact incident location using an interactive map.
Citizens attach photos for better issue verification.
Automatic assignment to relevant municipal department.
WebSocket notifications when complaint status changes.
Citizens can view nearby reported issues to avoid duplicates.
Authorities manage, assign, and update complaints.
Emergency complaints receive higher priority routing.
Filter complaints by area, type, or status.
Heatmaps, resolution time charts, and department performance reports.
Complaints automatically escalate if unresolved within deadline.
Works across desktop and mobile devices.
